title: Siege of Beirut
author_or_org: Wikipedia summary (used only as a pointer to quoted casualty literature)
strengths: Provides a clear siege window and a commonly cited civilian death range for the siege itself.
limitations: Tertiary source and not directly auditable here against the underlying printed reference.
notes: Search-result summary reports dates of 14 June-21 August 1982 and 4,000-5,000 Lebanese civilians killed during the siege.

title: 1982 Lebanon War
author_or_org: Wikipedia summary (used only as a pointer to casualty series attributed to Lebanese authorities and An Nahar)
strengths: Preserves two additional siege-relevant casualty series: 5,515 killed in the Beirut area and 6,775 dead in Beirut alone, with 84% civilians according to Lebanese authorities.
limitations: The geographies and time coverage are not identical to a clean West Beirut-only series, and the source itself is tertiary.
notes: Search-result summary states that An Nahar estimated 5,515 military and civilian deaths in the Beirut area from 6 June to 31 August, and that Lebanese authorities reported 6,775 dead in Beirut alone, 84% civilians.

title: United Nations document on the situation in Lebanon / West Beirut
strengths: Confirms that West Beirut was treated contemporaneously as a civilian-protection crisis distinct enough for UN alarm.
limitations: The accessible snippet does not provide a usable casualty denominator or numerator.
notes: Search-result summary says the UN was alarmed by the continued sufferings of Lebanese and Palestinian civilian populations in west Beirut.
